quoteFirst: In 1472 or 1473 a son was added to the family, and the parents had a horoscope taken of the child, who appeared at
quoteLast: on January 19, 1472, according to some; at forty-eight minutes past four in the afternoon of February 19, 1473, according to others; the exact instant of the nativity being an important point in astrological calculations, which, in those days, inspired in fathers and mothers the deepest concern.

quoteFirst: Whether Fudge’s watch was slow or whether, absorbed in their conversation, they consumed more time than they realized on the way, the City Hall clock proclaimed
quoteLast: when they reached the Common and, to Fudge’s intense disgust, the theater was out.
